mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate above 5.1% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, prompting risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.1%, or the signal's IC drops below -0.05 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and HOUST fails to explain > 15% of SPX return variance in 3-year rolling windows, or returns are negative despite both series rising.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 118.641 signals robust supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate margins.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 50% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 118.641, or the signal's IC falls below 0.02 in 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI reading above 194.1 indicates persistent inflation, prompting central bank hawkishness and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPI exceeds 194.1, or the signal's IC drops below -0.03 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals strong labor market health, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through elevated consumer spending.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 50% of periods with PAYEMS growth, or the signal's IC falls below 0.02 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in real sales (RSAFS) above 343,623 indicates strong consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate revenue outlooks.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 50% of instances where RSAFS exceeds 343,623, or the signal's IC drops below 0.03 in 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening cycle, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through reduced liquidity and higher borrowing costs.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the signal's IC falls below -0.03 in rolling 1-year windows.
